title: Northeastern University president’s compensation exceeds $3 million, records show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Northeastern University President Joseph Aoun's FY 2025 compensation exceeded $3 million, making him the highest-paid college leader in Massachusetts.
Aoun's salary increase was largely due to $700,000 deferred compensation and a $550,000 bonus in FY 2025.
MIT's President Sally Kornbluth was the second-highest paid, earning $2,151,788, nearly $1 million less than Aoun.

title: Cambridge man killed in early-morning shooting, investigators say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, of Cambridge was found dead from a gunshot wound near Broadway and Norfolk Street.
The shooting occurred shortly before 4:30 a.m.; Bautista's body was discovered by a pedestrian an hour later.
Cambridge police, state police, and the DA's office are investigating; information can be given to police anonymously.

title: New England Aquarium trainer joins effort to save endangered penguin chicks in South Africa
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Diana Major from New England Aquarium volunteered in South Africa to help rehabilitate endangered African penguin chicks.
African penguins have lost 97% of their population and may become functionally extinct by 2035 without intervention.
New England Aquarium supports conservation efforts in Africa, contributing through fieldwork and funding via its Marine Conservation Action Fund.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras apologizes for recent on-field confrontations: ‘It’s been a really tough week’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras apologizes for two on-field altercations, is appealing a seven-game MLB suspension.
Two deadly earthquakes in Venezuela have emotionally impacted Contreras, affecting his performance and behavior.
Contreras seeks to prove himself and possibly secure a 2026 MLB All-Star spot despite recent turmoil.

title: Red Sox Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected to MLB All-Star Game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Aroldis Chapman and Ranger Suarez selected for the 2026 MLB All-Star Game.
Aroldis Chapman has a 2.10 ERA, 33 strikeouts, and 17 saves across 25.2 innings this season.
Ranger Suarez has a 2.94 ERA and 92 strikeouts in 88.2 innings, leading Boston's young pitching staff.

title: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown after trade from Celtics: ‘This is what keeps you hungry’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Paul Pierce encourages Jaylen Brown post-trade, saying feeling disrespected can fuel his future success.
Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers, leading to criticism of Brad Stevens.
Pierce expressed disappointment in the trade but remains optimistic about Boston's prospects with Paul George.

title: Photos: Boston celebrates the Fourth of July
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston celebrated the Fourth of July with a parade and a reading of the Declaration of Independence at the Old State House.
Festivities continued on the Esplanade with the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring a drone show and performances by Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper.
Mayor Michelle Wu and Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey attended the events, which were part of the U.S. 250th anniversary celebrations.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs (7) over the last 30 days.
Durbin's turnaround started mid-season with a new personal hitting coach improving his batting average to .321.
The Red Sox have team control of Durbin until 2031, giving roster flexibility for future free agency moves.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Aroldis Chapman set the MLB relief pitcher strikeout record with his 1,364th strikeout in Red Sox win over the Angels.
Chapman surpassed Hall of Famer Hoyt Wilhelm's record in only 847 innings compared to Wilhelm's 1,872.1 innings.
Despite Chapman's record-breaking performance, he may be traded as Boston has a 13% chance to make the playoffs.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston is ranked as the most collision-prone city in the U.S., with drivers experiencing crashes every 3.7 years.
Worcester and Springfield rank among the bottom five, with drivers averaging over five years between collisions.
Providence is also among the riskiest, ranking 194th, while Brownsville, TX, is the safest with nearly 15 years between crashes.
